steps.title: Simplify both sides
steps.content:
Expand the left side: $\frac{12x+28}{4} = \frac{12x}{4} + \frac{28}{4} = 3x + 7$, so it becomes $3x + 7 - \frac{s}{13}$.

Expand the right side: $r(x-8) = rx - 8r$.

The equation is now:
$$3x + 7 - \frac{s}{13} = rx - 8r$$

steps.title: Set conditions for infinitely many solutions
steps.content:
A linear equation has infinitely many solutions when both sides are identical—the same expression in $x$. Rearrange by moving all terms with $x$ to the left:
$$3x - rx = -8r - 7 + \frac{s}{13}$$
$$(3 - r)x = -8r - 7 + \frac{s}{13}$$
For this to be true for all $x$, the coefficient of $x$ must be zero AND the constant on the right must be zero:
Condition 1: $3 - r = 0 \Rightarrow r = 3$
Condition 2: $-8r - 7 + \frac{s}{13} = 0$

steps.title: Substitute and solve for s
steps.content:
Substitute $r = 3$ into Condition 2:
$$-8(3) - 7 + \frac{s}{13} = 0$$
$$-24 - 7 + \frac{s}{13} = 0$$
$$-31 + \frac{s}{13} = 0$$
$$\frac{s}{13} = 31$$
$$s = 31 \times 13 = 403$$
Check: With $s = 403$ and $r = 3$, the left side is $3x + 7 - 31 = 3x - 24$ and the right side is $3(x-8) = 3x - 24$. Both sides are identical, confirming infinitely many solutions.
